name: THE HERO HUNTER — MARTIAL EVOLUTION PROTOCOL
fantasy: Learn every style, get faster with each fight. Build like a fighter-sprinter — lean, explosive, quick hands — and 'evolve' the way a martial artist really does: through repetition, speed, and conditioning, not a monster transformation.
core_rule: Speed and explosiveness are trained fresh and capped — never chase a 'one-punch' knockout or train through pain.
core_rule_enforcement: Every session starts with a warm-up (shoulders and wrists included for striking). Explosive work is quality-capped (plyometric foot-contacts, sprint volume) with full recovery and no back-to-back plyo days. Striking is speed-and-combination focused, not max-force chasing. Bag work uses wraps/gloves and progressive contact. The honest goal is a lean, fast, well-conditioned striker — not cartoon power.
mechanic: DUAL PATH (FIT / FIGHT / BOTH) + MARTIAL EVOLUTION. Each of the 10 stages can be run as FIT-only (the fighter-sprinter build), FIGHT-only (fast explosive striking), or BOTH combined. The FIGHT path 'learns a new style' each stage (boxing -> Dutch kickboxing -> Wing Chun -> med-ball power -> conditioning -> contrast -> integration), building speed and combinations. The FIT path builds the lean, explosive engine that actually drives fast striking: jump rope / plyometrics, explosive legs, med-ball throws, shoulder resilience, and sprint conditioning. Cardio stages fold in shadowboxing + plyometrics; striking stages fold in explosive bodyweight (burpees, tuck jumps, side lunges).
sourcing_note: Grounded in the deep-research report (protocol-src/research/garou-research-report.md — 107-agent multi-source deep research, adversarially verified, 21 confirmed / 4 refuted). WHAT IS REAL AND USED: (1) plyometrics/jump-rope/ballistic lower-body work transfers to faster punch speed, punch reaction time, and rate of force development — jump rope is a viable minimal-equipment plyometric substitute; (2) LOWER-body strength (not upper-body) is the trainable driver of punch force — program explosive legs; (3) upper-body plyometrics and medicine-ball throws add ballistic/throwing power (a striking proxy); (4) contrast/complex (variable-resistance) training improves punch speed/force/power — but it is an ADVANCED method, not for beginners; (5) safe plyo dosing ~50-200 foot-contacts/session (beginner 60-100), 48h moderate / 72h high recovery, no back-to-back plyo days. HONESTY / FANTASY FLAGS (from the research): plyometrics reliably improves punch SPEED, rate of force development, and power-ENDURANCE but does NOT reliably raise raw single-shot 'one-punch' max force — so anime 'one-punch power', mid-fight 'evolving', and monster transformation are FANTASY (flavor only), while fast, explosive, conditioned striking is genuinely trainable. Spot reduction is a MYTH: ab work builds the muscle but does not reveal it; visible abs come from whole-body fat loss driven by energy balance/nutrition (moderate aerobic = Grade A fat loss, moderate-high resistance training = Grade A lean-mass preservation) — you cannot out-train a poor diet. NOT ESTABLISHED BY THE VERIFIED RESEARCH (built from standard coaching convention and flagged as such): the striking-TECHNIQUE content itself (boxing/Dutch-kickboxing/Wing-Chun fundamentals, chain punching, trapping, centerline), reaction/reflex drills, shoulder/rotator-cuff injury-resilience protocols, and hand/wrist protection for bag work — these follow conventional safe coaching practice, not graded evidence. Two research sources (boxingfitness.com, performancepurpose.ca) were security-flagged during the run for probing the agent proxy status endpoint and were EXCLUDED from synthesis.
fit_focus: Lean, defined physique (via whole-body fat loss + muscle preservation, NOT spot reduction)
Explosive legs (the driver of punch power)
fight_focus: Solo shadowbox + bag work (no partner required)

normal.label: HERO HUNTER
normal.rest: moderate, full recovery preserved for max efforts

hard.label: AWAKENED
hard.rest: shorter between accessory work; full recovery for max sprints/plyo is never traded away
hard.note: Beginner plyo band is 60-100 foot-contacts (research). Hard sits at the TOP of the beginner band, never above. Contrast/complex training is unlocked only at the advanced stage (Stage 8).

hard_never_requires: Plyometric foot-contacts above the beginner cap (100)
Back-to-back plyometric days (48h moderate / 72h high recovery minimum)
Skipping the warm-up (incl. shoulders/wrists for striking)
Striking or jumping through wrist, shoulder, or joint pain

style_progression.boxing: Stages 1-3 — stance, guard, jab/cross/hook, footwork, relaxed speed.
style_progression.dutch_kickboxing: Stage 4 — boxing-forward combinations into low kicks and teeps.
style_progression.wing_chun: Stage 5 — centerline, chain punching, trapping (solo drills; conventional, not research-graded).
style_progression.power_and_conditioning: Stages 6-7 — med-ball power, power-endurance rounds, sprint + shadowbox conditioning.
style_progression.integration: Stages 9-10 — deload, then the Hero Hunter finale tying every style together.

safety_scaffolding.why: The real risks here are overuse from explosive volume (plyo/sprint), and wrist/hand and shoulder injury from bag work. The research supports explosive training for speed but caps volume and recovery; the striking-safety specifics follow conventional coaching practice.
rules: WARM UP FIRST, ALWAYS — including shoulder (scapular / rotator-cuff) and wrist prep before any striking. High-intensity work waits until the warm-up is done.
PLYO CAPS + RECOVERY: ~50-200 foot-contacts/session (beginner 60-100), 48h between moderate plyo sessions and 72h between high-intensity ones, and NO back-to-back plyometric days.
SPRINT QUALITY, FULL RECOVERY: max-effort sprints are low-volume/high-quality with full recovery between reps.
STRIKE FOR SPEED, NOT MAX FORCE: train fast, relaxed combinations and power-endurance; do NOT chase a maximal 'one-punch' knockout (not trainable — see honesty note).
PROTECT HANDS + SHOULDERS: wrap hands and use gloves for bag work, progress contact gradually, keep the wrist aligned; never bare-knuckle a heavy bag.
CONTRAST TRAINING IS ADVANCED: variable-resistance/complex work only from Stage 8, after the base is built.

HONESTY ON LEANNESS: a lean, visible-abs look comes from whole-body fat loss (energy balance + nutrition) plus preserved muscle — NOT from ab exercises or spot reduction. You cannot out-train a poor diet.
user_facing_stop_criteria: Wrist, hand, or knuckle pain during bag work (stop, check wrap/alignment)
Shoulder pain or impingement during pressing/striking
Sharp or localized joint pain (ankle, knee, elbow)
Shin pain building during running / sprint work
Dizziness, lightheadedness, chest pain, or shortness of breath
safety_scaffolding.stop_action: Any stop criterion halts the session immediately with a plain-language caution (stop, rest/ice; persistent joint or tendon pain = seek medical advice) and applies no XP penalty.
safety_scaffolding.screening_note: Beginners start at STUDENT difficulty, keep to the low end of every cap, wrap hands for all bag work, and add volume slowly — surface this on first entry.
fantasy_flags: 'One-punch' knockout power — NOT trainable (max single-shot force barely moves); flavor only.
'Evolving' or transforming mid-fight, monster form — pure fiction; the real 'evolution' is skill + speed + conditioning over time.

equipment_profiles.rule: Fully doable with open space + a timer: bag work has a shadow route, jump rope substitutes for plyo, and sprints have an on-the-spot fallback. Wraps/gloves are strongly recommended whenever a heavy bag is used. No heavy barbell equipment required.

voice_guidance.policy: The timer SPEAKS the workout so the user never has to read the screen. Every movement is announced by name as it begins, with a short form/coach cue, a spoken countdown into work and rest, round start/end calls, rep or time counts, a halfway call, a final-10-seconds call, and the module's key safety cue. Combinations (FIGHT path) are called out loud ('jab, cross, hook — go').
voice_guidance.source_fields: Spoken lines are generated from each module's title, exercises[]/drills[], coach_note, rounds[].goal/length, and the campaign safety cues — no on-screen reading required.
voice_guidance.note: Shared voice-guidance contract (see specs/22-voice-guidance.md) — embedded in every campaign so the timer is fully audio-guided.

achievements.name: Fighter-Sprinter
achievements.trigger: Clear a stage as BOTH (fit + fight combined)

causes: Explosive work taken past the cap / to exhaustion
response: Quality over volume — full recovery between max efforts

causes: Chasing max force instead of speed
response: Re-warm-up shoulders/wrists; check wraps and wrist alignment
Reset to relaxed speed and clean combinations

safety_stop: Dizziness / chest pain / shortness of breath

final_stage.name: The Hero Hunter
final_stage.rule: The finale ties every style together — warm-up, fast-hands boxing combinations, a Dutch combo into kicks, a Wing Chun chain-punch burst, explosive legs, a capped plyo/med-ball finisher, and conditioning rounds. Fast, clean, quality-capped — NEVER to exhaustion or max-force chasing. Enter rested (Stage 9 was the deload).
final_stage.outcome: FINAL CLEAR -> victory screen + The Hero Hunter / Every Style achievements (and Fighter-Sprinter if run as BOTH).

art.art_note: Placeholder — an original hero-hunter / martial-evolution banner (silhouette, speed lines) would suit; avoid trademarked One Punch Man / Garou character art.
